topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Maya was scrolling through her feed, mindlessly flicking through posts from friends, when a familiar profile picture popped up. It was Mr. Harrison, her notoriously stern history teacher. Her thumb froze mid-swipe. A slow, almost imperceptible tightening began in her chest. She blinked, rereading the username. Then, a notification chimed. It was a comment on a post she’d shared earlier that week—a picture of a particularly challenging essay. The comment read: “Excellent analysis, Maya. Keep up the great work.” Maya’s breath hitched. She quickly minimized the app, her fingers trembling slightly as she stared at her phone, trying to process the unexpected digital acknowledgment.

[story 2]

Liam had been expecting a reminder about the upcoming midterm review, but instead, his laptop screen lit up with an alert from an unfamiliar platform. He leaned closer, squinting at the notification. It was a direct message from Ms. Evans, his calculus instructor. The message wasn't about homework; it was a photo of a local coffee shop, captioned simply, “Enjoying a much-needed break.” Liam pulled back from the screen, a sudden stiffness creeping into his shoulders. He tapped the message again, reading the casual tone, and a faint flush rose on his neck. He felt a strange, unsettled feeling bubble up, a quiet dissonance in his usual routine.

[story 3]

For weeks, Chloe had been meticulously avoiding checking her notifications, determined to keep her focus solely on her studies. That was until she stumbled upon it while looking for an old assignment link. A profile picture of Professor Davies, the notoriously reserved literature professor, was prominently displayed on her feed. Her jaw dropped. She slowly lowered her phone, her eyes wide, taking in the screen as if it were a foreign object. A wave of sheer bewilderment washed over her. She tapped the profile, her heart beginning a quick, uneven rhythm against her ribs. The casual presence of the professor in her personal digital space felt entirely out of sync with the formal atmosphere of the classroom.
